teachings.title: The dichotomy of control
teachings.teaching: Some things are up to you — your judgments, your effort, your response. Most are not — outcomes, other people, the past. Spend yourself only on what is yours to move.
teachings.practice: Name the one thing in this situation that is actually up to you today. Act on that. Set the rest down.

teachings.title: Amor fati
teachings.teaching: Do not wish events to be as you want. Wish them to be as they are, and work from there. What happened is now material, not an insult.
teachings.practice: Take today's setback as the assignment. Ask: given this, what's the next right move?

teachings.title: Premeditatio malorum
teachings.teaching: Rehearse difficulty before it arrives. The blow you have already imagined lands softer; the temptation you've named loses its ambush.
teachings.practice: Name where today is most likely to knock you off. Decide your response now, while you're level.

teachings.title: The impediment to action advances action
teachings.teaching: What blocks the path becomes the path. The mind adapts and turns every obstacle to its own use. What stands in the way becomes the way.
teachings.practice: Point at the thing you're avoiding. That's the rep. Start there.

teachings.title: Character is the only thing fully yours
teachings.teaching: Wealth, reputation, and comfort come and go on their own terms. How you act — honestly, with discipline, without excuse — is the one thing no one can take.
teachings.practice: Judge today by one question: did you keep your word to yourself? Not by how you felt.

teachings.title: Memento mori
teachings.teaching: You could leave life right now. Let that determine what you do and say. The point is not fear — it's to stop wasting the day on things that don't matter.
teachings.practice: One thing matters most today. Do that first, before the noise.

teachings.title: Spot the distortion
teachings.teaching: Strong feelings ride on distorted thoughts: all-or-nothing ('I blew it, so it's ruined'), catastrophizing, mind-reading, overgeneralizing from one miss.
teachings.practice: Write the hot thought. Label the distortion in it. Naming it drains most of its charge.

teachings.title: Test the thought
teachings.teaching: A thought is a hypothesis, not a verdict. Ask what the actual evidence for and against it is, then write the more balanced, accurate version.
teachings.practice: One missed day is data, not a definition. Evidence against 'I always quit'? List two times you didn't.

teachings.title: Behavioral activation
teachings.teaching: Motivation follows action, not the other way around. Waiting to feel like it keeps you stuck; doing the small version of it changes how you feel.
teachings.practice: Shrink the task until it's almost too small to refuse. Do that version now. Momentum is the goal.

teachings.title: The ABC model
teachings.teaching: It isn't the event (A) that sinks you but the belief about it (B) that drives the consequence (C). Change the belief and the consequence changes.
teachings.practice: Event: you slipped. Belief: 'so why bother.' Swap the belief for 'so I log today and restart the count.'

teachings.title: Drop the 'shoulds'
teachings.teaching: 'I should be further along' is a rod you beat yourself with, and it never produces action — only guilt. Trade the should for a plan.
teachings.practice: Rewrite one 'I should' as 'I will [small thing] by [time] today.'

teachings.title: Return to now
teachings.teaching: Most suffering lives in a replayed past or a rehearsed future. The present is usually workable. Attention is a muscle: notice it wandered, bring it back.
teachings.practice: Three slow breaths. Name five things you can see. You're back. Now take the next action.

teachings.title: You are not the thought
teachings.teaching: A craving or a doubt is a passing event in the mind, not a command and not you. Watch it arise, crest, and pass without acting on it.
teachings.practice: Label it: 'urge.' Set a timer for ten minutes and just watch it. Most urges peak and fade if you don't feed them.

teachings.title: Equanimity
teachings.teaching: Meet the good and the hard with the same steady attention. You don't have to like what's happening to stop fighting the fact that it's happening.
teachings.practice: Say what's true without judgment: 'This is hard right now.' Then breathe once and take one useful step.

teachings.title: The breath is the anchor
teachings.teaching: When the mind spins, the breath is always here to come back to. It's not about clearing the mind — it's about noticing you left, and returning, again and again.
teachings.practice: One minute. Count breaths to ten, start over. When you notice you've drifted, that noticing is the whole exercise.

teachings.title: The control test
teachings.teaching: Before you spend a feeling on it, sort it: is this mine to move, or not? Modern Stoic practice keeps a running line between the two and acts only on the first.
teachings.practice: Split the situation into two columns — mine / not mine. Act only on the left one today.
teachings.source: Modern Stoicism — the dichotomy of control, applied

teachings.title: Rehearse the loss
teachings.teaching: Picture losing what you rely on — not to dread it, but to stop taking it for granted and to plan for the knock before it lands.
teachings.practice: Name one thing that could go wrong today. Decide your response now, while you're level.

teachings.title: View from above
teachings.teaching: Pull the camera back: this problem, set against your whole life and everyone alive — how big is it now? Perspective is a tool you can pick up on purpose.
teachings.practice: Zoom out once. Then do the one small thing actually in front of you.
teachings.source: Modern Stoicism — the view from above

teachings.title: A thought is not an order
teachings.teaching: You can notice a thought — 'I'll fail' — without obeying it. Put 'I'm having the thought that…' in front of it and its grip loosens.
teachings.practice: Name the loudest thought as a thought. Then act on your values, not on it.

teachings.title: Move toward what matters
teachings.teaching: Feelings come and go; values are a direction you keep choosing. Ask what kind of person you want to be here, then take one step that way.
teachings.practice: Name the value under this. Take one small action toward it now.

teachings.title: Make room for it
teachings.teaching: Fighting a hard feeling doubles it. Make room for the discomfort and keep doing the thing that matters — that's acceptance, not surrender.
teachings.practice: Drop the rope. Let the feeling be there while you take the next useful step.

teachings.title: This too will pass
teachings.teaching: Every state — the craving, the mood, the win — is already changing. Trying to hold it still is where the suffering is.
teachings.practice: Notice the urge rise. Watch it crest and fall without acting on it.
teachings.source: Dhammapada (trans. Bhikkhu Sujato, CC0 / public domain)

teachings.title: Mind made
teachings.teaching: What you repeat, you become; the deed starts as intention. Set the intention cleanly and the action follows it.
teachings.practice: Before the task, name the intention behind it in one honest sentence.

teachings.title: Loosen the grip
teachings.teaching: Suffering rides on clinging — to outcomes, to being right, to how it should be. Loosen the grip and you can still act, just without the fever.
teachings.practice: Name what you're clinging to. Do the next right thing anyway, holding it lightly.

teachings.title: Effortless action
teachings.teaching: Force creates resistance; water wears the stone by yielding. Act with the grain of the situation, not against it.
teachings.practice: Find where you're forcing. Stop pushing there and look for the opening instead.
teachings.source: Tao Te Ching (trans. James Legge, 1891, public domain)

teachings.title: Empty to be useful
teachings.teaching: The cup is useful because it's empty; the room, because of the space in it. Strip away the extra and what remains works.
teachings.practice: Cut one thing off today's list that isn't yours to carry.
teachings.source: Tao Te Ching, ch. 11 (trans. Legge, public domain)

teachings.title: The soft overcomes the hard
teachings.teaching: What is flexible survives; what is rigid breaks. Bend where you can so you don't snap where you can't.
teachings.practice: Pick the one thing worth being rigid about. Yield on the rest.
teachings.source: Tao Te Ching, ch. 76 (trans. Legge, public domain)

teachings.title: Act, release the fruit
teachings.teaching: Your right is to the work, never to its results. Do the duty in front of you fully, and let go of what the outcome will be.
teachings.practice: Do the next task for its own sake. Decide now you'll accept whatever result comes.

teachings.title: Even-minded in both
teachings.teaching: Meet success and failure with the same steady mind. That evenness — not the outcome — is the discipline itself.
teachings.practice: Name today's likely win and likely loss. Resolve to meet both the same way.

teachings.title: Be your own ally
teachings.teaching: The mind is the friend of the one who has mastered it, and the enemy of the one who hasn't. You choose which today.
teachings.practice: Win one small argument with yourself — do the thing you said you'd do.

teachings.title: Do not be anxious about tomorrow
teachings.teaching: Tomorrow will worry about itself; today has enough of its own. Steady the heart and do the next right thing in good faith.
teachings.practice: Set tomorrow down. Do the one duty today is actually asking of you.

teachings.title: Strength that serves
teachings.teaching: Count others' needs alongside your own and the ego quiets. Strength is proven in service, not in being seen.
teachings.practice: Do one unseen thing for someone today, expecting nothing back.

teachings.title: Set down the debt
teachings.teaching: Carrying a grievance costs you more than the one who wronged you. Forgiveness is releasing a debt you were never going to collect.
teachings.practice: Name one resentment. Decide to stop charging interest on it.

teachings.title: Sabr — steady under strain
teachings.teaching: Patience isn't passivity; it's holding your composure and your effort steady through hardship, without complaint.
teachings.practice: In the next frustration, pause once before you react. Hold steady, then respond.

teachings.title: Shukr — count what you have
teachings.teaching: Gratitude multiplies what is given and steadies the heart against the pull of always wanting more.
teachings.practice: Name three things you have right now that you'd hate to lose. Then begin the day.

teachings.title: Tie your camel, then trust
teachings.teaching: Do everything in your power, then release the outcome. The effort is yours to give; the result is not yours to hold.
teachings.practice: Give the full effort on one thing today. Then hand the result over and rest.
teachings.source: On trust (tawakkul); 'tie your camel' — hadith, Jami' at-Tirmidhi

teachings.title: Sort your wants
teachings.teaching: Some desires are natural and necessary; some are just loud. Cut the loud ones and contentment gets cheap.
teachings.practice: Name one craving today that's manufactured, not needed. Let it pass unfed.

teachings.title: Enough is a feast
teachings.teaching: Pleasure is the absence of pain and disturbance, not endless more. What you already have, seen clearly, is often enough.
teachings.practice: Before adding anything today, ask what you already have that's enough.

teachings.title: Guard your people
teachings.teaching: Of all that wisdom gathers for a good life, the greatest by far is friendship. Tend it before you need it.
teachings.practice: Reach out to one person who steadies you. Don't wait for a reason.

teachings.title: Condemned to be free
teachings.teaching: No script is handed to you; you are the sum of your choices. That is the weight and the freedom both.
teachings.practice: Stop waiting for permission. Make one choice today you've been deferring.

teachings.title: Own it, don't outsource it
teachings.teaching: Bad faith is hiding behind 'I had no choice.' Authenticity is owning that you did choose, and choosing on purpose.
teachings.practice: Catch one 'I had to' today. Rewrite it as 'I chose to' — and mean it.

teachings.title: Make the meaning
teachings.teaching: The universe won't hand you a why. You build it by what you commit to and keep repeating.
teachings.practice: Name one thing worth doing even if no one notices. Do a piece of it today.
